title: QUIC RFC 9001 Large Key-Update Epoch Work Item
summary: Remove the artificial 32-bit repeated key-update epoch ceiling from the internal runtime model while preserving existing one-bit Key Phase behavior.
planned_changes:
- Add the canonical RFC 9001 Section 6.5 requirement for internal repeated key-update epochs wider than the one-bit packet Key Phase signal.
- Widen repeated 1-RTT key-update epoch identifiers in the bridge, lifecycle, sender ledger, congestion-control ledger, and recovery ledger.
- Remove the repeated local and peer install guard that rejected updates at the previous 32-bit maximum.
- Add requirement-home tests that install repeated local and peer updates across the old 32-bit boundary and prove lifecycle cleanup state keeps the wide retained-old epoch.
- Keep public support claims narrow and avoid broad key-update API or interop promises.
out_of_scope: A general unbounded key-update ledger redesign
verification_plan: Run the focused RFC 9001 S6P5 large-epoch requirement-home tests, rerun adjacent repeated key-update ownership and acknowledgment tests, build the solution, run SpecTrace core validation and Workbench core validation, and finish with git diff --check before committing.
completion_notes: Completed for the narrow large internal epoch identifier slice. The old 32-bit repeated-update guard is removed, sender/recovery cleanup follows the wide retained-old epoch, and broader unbounded key-update lifecycle support remains outside this slice.
